# Pull Request Template

Thank you for contributing to the **Emergency Department Flow Simulator**! Please fill out the sections below to help us review your changes efficiently.

## Description

Provide a clear and concise description of the changes or additions in this pull request. Explain the purpose and impact of your contribution.

- **What does this PR do?** (e.g., fixes a bug, adds a feature, improves documentation)
- **Why is this change needed?** (e.g., addresses an issue, enhances performance)
- **Related Issues**: Link to any relevant GitHub issues (e.g., `Fixes #123`).

## Changes Made

List the specific changes made, such as:
- Modified files or directories (e.g., `app.py`, `Phase_3.py`).
- New features, bug fixes, or documentation updates.
- Updates to visualizations or output files (e.g., `los_distribution.png`).

## Testing

Describe how you tested your changes:
- **Environment**: Python version, OS, and dependencies used.
- **Steps**: How did you verify the changes (e.g., ran `briefcase dev`, checked KPIs)?
- **Results**: Confirm that the simulation runs without errors and meets expected outcomes (e.g., throughput, LOS).

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the project’s coding guidelines (PEP 8 for Python).
- [ ] I have tested my changes thoroughly and confirmed no regressions.
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation (e.g., `README.md`, `Phase_*.md`).
- [ ] My changes are compatible with Python 3.9+ and project dependencies.
- [ ] I have added or updated visualizations/output files if applicable.
- [ ] My commit messages are clear and descriptive.
- [ ] I have reviewed the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/VoxDroid/EDFlowSimulator/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## Screenshots (if applicable)

Include screenshots of GUI changes, new visualizations, or other visual updates.

## Additional Notes

Add any other context or information relevant to the review process (e.g., trade-offs, future improvements).

---

**Note**: Ensure your pull request aligns with the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/VoxDroid/EDFlowSimulator/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). Maintainers will review your PR within 3–5 days and provide feedback.
